cleavage agent: subjected to enzymatic digestion with sequencing-grade trypsin (Promega, Madison, WI, USA)
fragmentation method: generate fragment ions by higher energy collisional dissociation (HCD, NCE:25%)
instrument: using a Dionex Ultimate 3000 RSLC nanoUPLC (Thermo Fisher Scientific Inc, Waltham, MA, USA) system and a Q Exactive Orbitrap mass spectrometer
ionization type: by matrix-assisted laser desorption ionisation–time of flight (MALDI-TOF) mass spectrometry
labeling: Label-free quantification (LFQ) mass spectrometry experiments indicated that the abundances of endogenous EXT1 and EXT2 were 10,000–100,000-fold less than that of EXTL3ΔN
reduction reagent: Protein solutions were reduced (DTT) and alkylated (iodoacetamide)
